message:
Round 21: Array-based split_table (8% gain, revealed real issue)

Implemented: HashMap → Array for split_table
Result: 8% improvement (6.3s → 5.8s on 1000 iterations)

Changes:
- split_table: HashMap(u32, Pair) → []Pair
- Direct array access instead of HashMap .get()
- 800KB array, densely packed (100,256 entries)

Performance (1000 iter):
- PyAOT: 5.814s (User: 3.154s, System: 2.534s)
- rs-bpe: 4.396s (User: 4.036s, System: 0.065s)

CRITICAL DISCOVERY:
- PyAOT user time: 3.154s (FASTER than rs-bpe 4.036s!)
- PyAOT system time: 2.534s (44% - KILLER!)
- rs-bpe system time: 0.065s (1.6% - normal)

If we eliminate 2.5s system overhead:
- PyAOT would be 3.15s vs rs-bpe 4.40s = 28% FASTER!

Real bottleneck: System calls (malloc/free, file I/O, or threading)
Next: Identify and fix system overhead source
